mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation reduces real corporate earnings and increases discount rates, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL declines are accompanied by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations over three years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumption and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE above 4.5% is followed by positive 20-day returns in over half of triggered instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad economic contraction, which depresses corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST do not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than single-series declines (Mann-Whitney test, p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth exceeds 3% year-over-year, it signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS above threshold leads to negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases over any two-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS above 4.5% yields positive 5-day returns in over 50% of instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth signals a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If declining PAYEMS is associated with positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases over a three-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low GDP growth (below 2% YoY) and high inflation (CPI above 3% YoY) create a stagflation environment that severely depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If stagflation instances do not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than non-stagflation periods (t-test p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4%, the labor market is operating beyond full employment, creating wage inflation pressures that compress profit margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE below 4% is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any two-year period, reject.
